title: Cross-session cancel: customer returns in new browser session
failing_behavior: Customer booked in a previous session, returns, says 'cancel my appointment'. biz._lastBookingId and biz._bookingState.phone are empty (sessionStorage gone). Cancel handler falls through with no action. Customer is silently not cancelled or told to call the salon.
root_cause: Cancel handler (send() cancel branch) required either biz._lastBookingId OR biz._bookingState.phone. In a new session both are empty. Even if Claude asks for phone and customer provides it, the Firestore query had two additional bugs: (1) only searched 'confirmed' status (not 'in_progress'), (2) cancelled ALL matching docs instead of only the most recent booking.
fix_summary: Three changes: (1) Updated prompt MODIFY section to document cross-session handling. (2) Fixed cancel Firestore query: include in_progress status, take only most recent booking (sort by createdAt desc), also query legacy 'phone' field as fallback. (3) Existing prompt 'ask for name/phone if not in STATE' rule already handles the 2-turn cross-session ask — no prompt change needed for cancel.

conversation.content: Hi, I need to cancel my appointment

Of course! Could you confirm your name and phone number so I can locate your booking?
[STATE:{"intent":"cancel_booking","services":[],"staff":null,"date":null,"time":null,"name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

Of course, Jane — I've cancelled your appointment. Sorry we won't see you this time!
[STATE:{"intent":"cancel_booking","services":[],"staff":null,"date":null,"time":null,"name":"Jane Smith","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
[ESCALATE:cancel]

expected_firestore_action: cancel most recent confirmed or in_progress booking for customerPhone='4085551234'
notes: The 2-turn ask flow (Claude asks for phone, customer gives phone + escalate) was already partially working because _mergeState() populates biz._bookingState.phone before the cancel handler reads it. The real bugs were in the Firestore query: wrong status filter and cancelling all matches instead of most recent.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies fix strings in source. Live testing required to confirm: (1) Claude asks for phone correctly in new session, (2) [ESCALATE:cancel] fires after phone is collected, (3) Firestore cancel hits the correct booking doc.
